x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.111, p.014312 (2025)

x4freetext: ELBE
Irradiated by Ee=8.6 and 12.2 MeV electrons:
- Ee=8.6 MeV (560 uA) on Nb foil (5 um thick)
- Ee=12.2 MeV (670 uA) on Nb foil (12.5 um thick).
In Ee=12.2 MeV irradiation, low-energy part of the
bremsstrahlung spectrum was reduced by Al absorber
(10 cm thick) behind the Nb radiator.
- Natural cobalt (1.8379 g, 20 mm diameter)
- Enriched (95.5%) 11B (0.300 g, 20 mm diameter)
Four HPGe detectors with efficiency of
~100% relative to a NaI detector (7.6 cm diam.,
7.6 cm long) surrounded by BGO scintillation
detectors (3 cm thick).
- Two HPGe detectors at 127 deg relative to the photon
beam direction and at distance of 32 cm from the
target.
- Two HPGe detectors at 90 deg relative to the photon
beam direction and at distance of 28 cm from the
target.
Absorbers (8 mm Pb+3 mm Cu at 90 deg, 3 mm Pb+3 mm Cu
at 127 deg) were placed in front of HPGe detectors
to reduce the count rate due to low-energy photons.
Proof-read by Ronald Schwengner (2025-04-23)

The energy values was deduced from the gamma-ray
energy measured at 127 degree and corrected for
recoil and Doppler-shift.

x4headers.headerHelp: Value of quantity specified under REACTION
Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: Below an excitation energy of 7.0 MeV the value was
deduced from the measurement at Ee=8.6 MeV and for
higher excitation energies from the measurement at
Ee=12.2 MeV.
The energy values was deduced from the gamma-ray
energy measured at 127 degree and corrected for
recoil and Doppler-shift.
Transitions found in the Ee=8.6 MeV measurement were
assumed to be ground-state transitions, and
additional transitions observed up to 8.6 MeV in the
Ee=12.2 MeV measurement were considered to be
inelastic transition.

x4freetext: Absorption (scattering) cross section was derived by
dividing the (g,g0) elastic scattering cross section
by the ground state transition branching ratio.
An estimate of intensities of branching transitions to
excited states and the respective branching ratios of
the ground-state transitions was performed by
simulating statistical gamma-ray cascades using the
code gammaDEX.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: uncertainties including
- statistical uncertainties of the spectra
- uncertainties of the efficiencies and subtracted
simulated background
- spectra uncertainties of the flux resulting from the
integrated cross sections of the 11B levels
- uncertainties of the level-density parameters.
